narrative.text: Trading Upgrade Trial: Come up with a plan. UPDATE 05/05: No update on this, and NGO pointed out that this is a priority - it's important to make people aware of this & asked for confirmation of date for completion. UPDATE 11/5: Launched in SW stores. Results and lessons learnt to be brought to trade post Late Spring Bank Holiday. UPDATE 18/5: Stock of 'Trade-in Clubs stock continues to build (126 sticks in, 16 sold) and update to be given post Bank Holiday. UPDATE 26/5: SE commented that sales of the products traded in Wk1 was £700, Wk 2 doubled, & Wk 3 hit £2,700 so pleased & stores are happy with the process. SE has a meeting with TSP diarised. SE suggested that we don't roll this out to all stores at the same time & we need to work out how aggressive we are on this as we don't want a stock problem. SE is going to do some more work on this TW. We need to decide what region next. EW suggested to be more aggressive where there are other competitors. Meeting diarised for Thursday. EW suggested he Central Area went next, along with Edinburgh – all have strong competition. UPDATE 01/6: SE had a number of meetings LW and will come back with his findings next week. UPDATE 15/6:

Focus Areas: Various actions across Marketing & Digital to improve performance at New Malden and Guilford stores, and drive Package Set sales. LV & SF to share performance updates at the end of July. UPDATE 15/06: ECOMMERCE - Waiting on Video from Marketing so that we can execute Social & PPC demand gen video ads. Presenting at the end of July.

Fazer Package Sets: Fazer package sets in particular are under-performing. Adam to present back plan to drive sales. UPDATE 15/6: Wheels in motion on POS update in store. Also looking at what customers purchase packages sets so we can target the cohort digitally.

Local Marketing Tracking: To monitor success of local marketing activity for specific stores (promotional and partnerships), Finance will overlay sales performance against targeted stores during targeted dates, to measure performance uplift and to police levels of discount. UPDATE 15/06: See slides for detail. Partnership marketing generated 15 discount code uses across seven stores YTD, equating to £247 in discounts and five new customers. Due to the low volume, no further analysis has been completed. Local driving database results were mixed across four stores, with 3 Hammers showing the strongest improvement. Performance at the other stores was impacted by local factors, making the overall results inconclusive without customer-level analysis of database overlap.

Price Match: Should we be more aggressive with the Price Match message? DJ and LV to trial targeted email and social media ads/messages to drive Southampton store. Run for two weeks and review results to potentially roll out across all comms. UPDATE 15/6: MARKETING - First email calling out the price match promise for Hedge End was sent Monday 8th June. It was agreed with Chris Roughley and communicate in trade last week that would need 4 weeks before worth running any impact analysis. Ecomm/Liam to confirm when targeted Facebook ads can be run. ECOMMERCE - Waiting on Marketing have not yet supplied any content for us to execute the Social campaigns.

Retail: Support stores in driving custom fit bookings, using warmer weather and increased golf participation as the trigger to bring customers into store. UPDATE 15/6: RETAIL: Increase Online Bookings for Stores: Aaron has kindly trialled doubling budget on paid social promotions on North and South West to monitor whether there is a noticeable increase. This will be over a two-week trial period and was actioned Monday 8th. ECOMMERCE - We have trialled doubling spend across half of the regions to test the impact. Ongoing. Results not in yet.

Ecommerce: Review sessions versus budget and optimise spend while staying within this week's agreed budget. UPDATE 15/6: ECOMMERCE - We increased spend to drive sessions LW. Demand is the issue, so this action drove up COS above target.

Marketplace: Hold meeting to review Slick Stitch commercials. UPDATE 15/6: SS reviewed performance with Inder following the paid ads switch-off. Sales are down 47% YoY, and the current commission model doesn't make ad spend commercially viable. Inder requested a monthly ad spend breakdown to assess a potential contribution, though expectations are low. Reiterated the long-term plan to migrate to the AG website, with a sustainable commercial model needed in the interim to prevent further sales decline.

Regional Managers: Increase EA orders and drive performance above 90%. UPDATE: 15/6: Dump Bin Review / IPB increase support: Ryan is creating a PBI model to help understand currently how much sales are driven from the SKUs in dump bins at the moment. Once we have enough data, a meeting will be chaired for suggestions on what product can be bought for next year. Short term Roisin has made a more compelling offer for RIFE/Ladies Stromberg gloves and stores have moved them closer to the till to see if it helps overall IPB performance in store.
New Golfers: Review why new golfer performance is 5.9% behind budget YTD and propose actions to close the gap. UPDATE 15/6: Will report back NW.

E-mail attribution: Contact Glass Atlas to understand the GA4 email attribution issue and confirm whether the 11% attribution is accurate. UPDATE 15/6: MARKETING - Glass Atlas confirmed that there isn't an attribution issue – if they can identify the source is email/another digital channel then GA4 is attributing correctly. The problem is identifying the correct channel and possible issues with the tags firing correctly which is sitewide. Site speed and performance remain outstanding tickets and need support from ecom to confirm when these will be addressed. ECOMMERCE - Glass have confirmed "we cant see an attribution issue, there is a potential issue with tag firing but that is a sitewide issue and isn't just for email and the discrepancy between clicks and sessions is an expected variance"

Marketing Brand: Share and review full brand survey results next week, with particular focus on how to build stronger customer connection to the brand. UPDATE 15/6: The high-level summary has been circulated to the directors, but additional deep dive follows are required to understand more about brand perception. This will be presented back once we have all the information.

Partnership discounts manually entered into infocode via tills in store. Across the 7 stores, there have been 15 uses identified since Feb, giving a discount of £247. 5 uses were from New Customers, 3 from reactivated. Given the low usage, no further analysis has been conducted. Specific activity has also been at Blanch re Ladies Golf – since that activity there has been minimal movement post vs pre on Ladies clothing sales.

3 Hammers has shown significant growth of +10.7%pts from pre to post activity. This outperforms central region growth of +2.0%, store type decline of (2.1%) and grade decline of (3.0%) over the same timeframe.

Hambrook has shown a decline of (5.3%pts) from pre to post activity, whilst other comparable stores have shown increases over the same period LFL. Note it is still trading 18.5% higher LFL, hence not a cause for concern.

A1 shows a decline of (38%pts) from pre to post activity however is still +40% up LFL. The Region mvt is down (5.2%), store type +7.9% and grade +1.9%. It has been noted there have been staffing issues at this site last year. YTD customers are up 22% (retained +25%, reactivated +37%.). Store closures of Trent Park and Hendon have also benefited the store.
New Malden has shown a decline of (25%) from pre to post activity and is trading (11.9%) down LFL. Other stores in the region are up 3.3%, and store type up 2.3. New customers are down 29% YOY YTD.
Summary: 3Hammers has seen a positive increase in LFL since commencement of activity (and outperforms all comparators), however the other stores are inconclusive given wider factors at play.

Dump Bin Summary: As above – Bin 2 (socks) generates the most revenue and performed particularly well in P11 FY26 (presumably stocking fillers). Overall LTM dump bin sales amounts to £99.6k, 55% of which is from Bin 2 (socks). Attachment rate of all bin items over LTM averages at 1.1%, with very little variation between regions and store types. In more recent weeks / months, attachment rate and stock holding have been trending downwards below the LTM average. There is also very little variation in the attachment rates between the three bins.

Clubs are (10%) vs Bud L4W, with a sales upside/(Risk) of (£1,056k) N6W. Custom risk of (£247k), Non custom Irons risk of (£258k) and woods risk of (£320k). Packages also poses a (£219k) risk based on RR.
Equipment is (9%) vs Bud, giving a sales upside/(risk) of (£481k) N6W. Balls carrying the largest risk.
Clothing is (4%) vs Bud, giving sales (risk)/upside of (£19k).
Footwear overall (13%) vs Bud L4W, giving an (risk)/upside of (£154k).
Based on the L4Ws trading and variances to Budget, with margin rate being +0.7%pts vs Bud, the run rate cash margin +upside/(risk) is (£590k).
Clubs M% is (0.1%pts) vs bud – with cash margin (risk)/upside of (£435k).
Equipment is 0.3%pts vs BUD – with a cash margin N6W (risk)/upside of (£166k). NB Garmin SOR factored into month end numbers.
Clothing is +1.9%pts – with a cash margin N6W (risk)/upside of £47k – based on L4W sales run rate.
Footwear is +3.3%pts, with a cash margin (risk) / upside of (£37k).

YOY lower number of occurrences of transactions on IFC, which include Blue Light discount in FY27, relative to FY26
FY27 Weeks 10 through to 16 has seen a spike in the number of transactions with both facilities applied
L6W - 165 transactions with net sales value of £32k. Using average IFC cost per transaction of L6W of £55.94, this has cost an extra £9k on top of Blue Light Discount
Overall net sales amount of these transactions is £216k (From start of FY26 to date). C.48% are in stores based in the North, however the custom trial was initially launched in the North
Total sales through Ecom channel where both discounts applied from start of FY26 to date have been £265k
As with retail, spike in total sales value in L6W for ecom sales, with transactions to value of £51k including both IFC and Blue Light
To put this into cost terms, using same average IFC cost of £55.94, there have been 113 orders in L6W with both discounts, costing c.£6.3k in IFC costs
Accident A1 - Minor: Staff member was moving an electric trolley from the display stand and felt a pain in their ankle, resulting in a sprained Achilles, investigations are ongoing

Accident Barnehurst - Minor: Player on course was struck by a golf ball, resulting in a bruise to the shoulder, investigations are ongoing, initial findings indicate that the ball came from the first tee

H&S Key Update - Retail: Boucher Road - Following protests and unrest in Belfast during the week, the invacuation policy and plan was implemented and followed to ensure staff, customers and the store were safe and not at risk
H&S Key Update - Retail: Site visits - Chester and Netherley to discuss H&S inspections/checks and H&S issues with new managers
H&S Key Update - Retail - Trafford: Store visited and temporary risk assessments (Deliveries) reviewed and amended following ongoing construction works on site

H&S Key Update - RSC - Fire Safety: Further discussions with (ASDA/MAPP) regards the fire alarm panel, ownership of responsibilities for AG and neighbouring premises as well as policy and procedure with regards to evacuations and alarm activations (ASDA/MAPP)

NB - AGT net sales is cost +15% of the profit value per Slick Stitch contract. Slick Stitch net sales shown for illustrative purposes only, amounts recognised in AGT is the AGT net sales figure. Security alert raised from Area Managers in Teams, advising stores had been contacted by unknown party requesting access to IT systems to check for customer transactions that may be duplicate, or where customer had been charged twice.
Actions Taken: Advice email sent to stores to hang up if contacted by a suspicious caller following alert.

Remediation actions: Contacting all stores to confirm whether they were contacted and what the caller asked. As of Noon Monday 15th June 2026: 7 stores were contacted fraudulently. Around 30 stores remaining to contact. Investigating blocking the number calls originated from with suppliers.
Other Issues: 9 stores contacted as they had iPads that had not been seen on monitoring systems for over 30 days. One user queried an email from automated Phishing training, Phish.io.
The new Blanchardstown store opened FY26 Wk40.

narrative.status: Top Categories - Solved Tickets - Week 18 Highlights

narrative.text: V12 - Cancelled order process needs defining, Orders being placed without mandatory billing/shipping addresses being the same.

Drop ship Issues: Returns - unable to process refund in Nav; Drop Ship - issues with EU/Jersey shipping; WWG issues - orders not processing correctly, no visibility of orders or tracking information available to assist customers. CS having to manually email each order with tracking details currently

Endless Aisle Challenges: Store processing & accuracy issues - Late dispatch/incorrect items sent/tags left on/ clubs sent that are not in a pristine condition. Courier Collections - not scanning parcels when collected, leading to MIT orders.
Custom Online: Suppliers amending orders - OOS/component changes. Chasing brands for updates. Slow Replies. ETA's not accurate/Clear for web custom online

In the last week, customers most frequently discussed staff, product, service, golf, and retail. Positive feedback highlighted staff knowledge, helpfulness, and no-pressure sales, alongside valuable in-store testing facilities. Conversely, negative experiences stemmed from poor staff service, delivery delays, stock availability issues, and incorrect pricing.

Total Customers: Vs budget significant drop from +2.6% to 0.6%. YoY also declined from +4.6% to +2.5%. Ecomm +26.7% YoY, Retail (4.4%) YoY
Frequency: No change in frequency vs budget but notable YOY change. Last week, total customer frequency was +1.7%, this week that has dropped to (0.2%) YOY
ATV: Improved WOW to +6.1% vs +5.8% vs budget
ACV: No notable change in the week. (0.5%) vs budget

£22k revenue, -59.4% vs budget. Revenue -51% vs FY25 (2 Yr actual). Last year Blue Flag up to 50% ending soon message. 2 years ago Father's Day deals ending FD

No strong individual email performance across the week. Fri-Sat emails amended due to delay in TrueFeel ball promo and weather but did not perform.
Store sign up, 2nd order discount (£10 off £70+) live from 4th June but only limited uses to date. Opportunity to roll out to online sign ups
Target for this week £90k, next week £63k. Looks like sale built into this weeks' forecast.
Week 20 Last Year Highlights: Blue Flag up to 50% off deals must end Wed 18th June; Balls multibuy discount/FJ 3 for 2 gloves; Sale launched up to 50% off 20th June
Commercial Focus (Wk20): Father's Day Gifting/deals end midnight Sunday; New Garmin deals; Polo deals; Balls including TruFeel double dozen promo; Adidas shoes co-op pot paid for solus

Customer Activations (Wk20): AG Club win £500 PGA bundle + Shot Scope LM1 with purchase of balls; AG Club email exclusive 6 hour flash 10% discount Wednesday evening; Social Media Prize (follow to win); New store sign-up 2nd order conversion incentive trial; World Cup Predictions Campaign

Partnerships & Reach (Wk20): Dave Jones partnership launch to support World Cup predictions activity (85k followers); Content creator support for Father's Day - Victoria Hearn (243k) and Leo Boniface (65k); GHD solus in support of Father's Day (134k - Tuesday 16th); Blue Light push for Father's Day - inclusion in top Father's Day offers homepage on app and web (running until Sunday 21st); PR gifting release - editorial drive

Local Marketing (Wk20): Email to support Hambrook/3 Hammers driving ranges - (40k) to support Father's Day gifting. Support for Thurrock store on Golf One socials (3k targeted to local area) every other day to support Father's Day gifting.

June Plan on a Page: Key Dates - World Cup Starts (11), US Open (18-21), Father's Day (21). Marketing Campaign - We're Obsessed Too; World Cup Prediction Campaign (14th June - 19th July). Trade Campaign - Father's Day; SALE (25th June). Events - Cobra Demo Days.
Benross x Todays Golfer - Targeted Paid Social Campaign (min 2,000 click KPI)

Organic Social Performance Overview: Father's Day gifting remained a key focus, supported by influencer content from @sophxharris (over 100k views, 300+ link clicks). We're Obsessed Too campaign driving range short-form video surpassed 92k views and 4.2k engagements. Callaway Opus wedge offer contributed to 85 units sold. US Open activity with four Callaway giveaway activations. Episode 1 of Get Into Golf YouTube series launched. World Cup content (Justin Rose, Dan Brown, Tubes, Ange, @georgiagolfcoach) reached additional 56k accounts. TikTok 3,917 new viewers (+48.8% WoW).
No till or BO failures in period. Three Navision issues affecting one customer order, and two till issues during trade.

Fluent Zero Stock Issues: Three issues at Trafford where orders received from Fluent with no stock available in store. Custom Fit - V12 Issues: Two stores affected by issues processing Custom Fit orders following payment with V12.
Custom Fit pc shipped to Hull to replace faulty Custom Fit laptop. Ongoing issue at Aintree with Trackman laptop.

feedback.quote: Customers consistently praise staff for their deep understanding of products and golf. This expertise helps customers make informed decisions and feel confident in their purchases.
Reviewers frequently highlight the staff's willingness to assist and guide them. This helpfulness contributes to a positive and supportive shopping experience.
Customers appreciate that staff provide advice and assistance without being pushy. This approach creates a comfortable environment, allowing customers to make decisions at their own pace.
The ability to try out clubs and equipment in-store, often with simulators, is a significant advantage. This hands-on experience helps customers confirm their choices before buying.
Staff are perceived as disinterested, unhelpful, or lacking product knowledge. This results in inadequate assistance and negative customer interactions.
Customers experience significant delays in receiving their orders, often exceeding a week. This leads to frustration and unfulfilled expectations regarding delivery times.
Desired items are frequently out of stock, both in-store and online. This limits customer choice and prevents purchases.
Customers are charged more than the marked price for items. This creates distrust and requires additional effort to resolve.
Rang customer services to be told send email requesting for you to ring me. too much writing to explain again
My white polo shirt was missing from my order
Still waiting on goods to be delivered.
I have yet to receive my purchase, in fact a week after payment my goods have not even been dispatched, and I have received no information regarding my goods even after sending an email
My order still hasn't arrived over 8 days later, and I've been asked to review it......
when i placed my order it stated free next day delivery however it has been a week and i am still waiting
The delivery service from EVRI is terrible I'm still waiting 7 days later still telling me it's not out for delivery yet.
Ordered balls which were delivered as expected. However I was using a £200 token i had won and asked for the delivery to be made up to or clise to that value. Never happened. Otherwise fine
The purchase was straight fwd enough online, but surprised regarding the delivery time. Took 2 weeks to dispatch
Delivery took too long, your competitors deliver in half the time which is a little frustrating
Normal customer service. Unfortunately one item could not be shipped
Ok service thanks
Online website very easy to work. The delivery speed by Evri has been very very poor sadly. Limited communication and after 1 week still no product
Variety of choice +Quality of products for the price.
Bought items with my birthday £10 off! Very pleased.
Got what I ordered in a timely fashion
Easy to order. Prompt delivery on time. Thank you.
Easy to order and quick delivery

summary: Headline: American Golf had a tough Week 19 — Total AGT despatched sales £2,844k, 16.9% behind budget and -17.5% LFL; MTD £6,112k is -12.5% vs budget. But this is a DEMAND problem, not a margin or product problem: margin rate held at 42.2% (+0.4pts vs budget) and Ecomm/Web is outperforming. Key movements: retail transactions fell sharply while ATV/ASP rose (losing footfall, not basket value); Web margin rate +2.8pts YoY with category sales well up YoY; the N6W run-rate flags a sales risk of c.£1.7m (Clubs the biggest at c.£1.06m) and cash-margin risk c.£0.59m. Watch items: two new stores (Blanchardstown -65%, Go Northampton -54% vs target YTD) are well behind; customer pain centres on delivery delays, stock availability and incorrect pricing despite a steady 4.7 TrustScore; a vishing fraud-call campaign hit 7 stores and needs operational follow-up.

insights.headline: Sales well behind budget — but it's volume, not product or margin
insights.detail: Total AGT despatched £2,844k vs £3,424k budget (-16.9%), -17.5% LFL. Yet margin rate held at 42.2% (+0.4pts vs bud). Retail transactions fell sharply while ATV and ASP rose — American Golf is losing footfall, not basket value. Fix demand, not pricing.

insights.headline: Ecommerce/Web is the bright spot — accelerate it
insights.detail: Web margin rate 40.7% (+2.8pts YoY) and L4W web category sales are well up year-on-year (Clubs/Equipment/Clothing all double-digit up). Ecomm MTD margin grew strongly. This is the channel to lean into while retail footfall recovers.

insights.headline: N6W run-rate risk ~£1.7m, concentrated in Clubs
insights.detail: The Category Summary N6W flags a sales risk/(upside) of c.(£1,711k), of which Clubs is c.(£1,056k) — Custom (£247k), Irons (£258k) and Woods (£320k) the largest. Cash-margin risk is c.(£590k) on the run rate. This is the number that threatens the forecast.

insights.headline: New stores badly behind target
insights.detail: Blanchardstown is -64.9% vs target YTD and Go Northampton -54.2%, both opened FY26. Chichester is the exception, running +15% ahead of target. The two Irish/SW openings are dragging the new-store contribution.

insights.headline: Customer experience risk: delivery, stock and pricing
insights.detail: TrustScore is steady at 4.7 (175k reviews, market-leading by volume), but negative themes are delivery delays (esp. EVRI), stock availability and incorrect pricing; 1-star reviews are dominated by late/non-delivery. CS handled 2,325 inbound contacts with dispatch/delivery/refunds the top tickets.

insights.headline: Security: vishing fraud-call campaign hit 7 stores
insights.detail: Fraudsters posing as IT support called stores requesting system access to check 'duplicate' transactions; 7 stores were contacted, ~30 still to verify. Advice issued to hang up. Needs operational confirmation across the estate.

insights.headline: Marketplaces declining; Slick Stitch under review
insights.detail: Total marketplace Wk19 £28.9k vs £39.8k budget; Slick Stitch sales -47% YoY with the current commission model making ad spend non-viable — long-term plan is migration to the AG website.

insights.headline: Custom fit holding up
insights.detail: Custom-fit appointments +14% LFL in Week 19 (in-store +19%) and custom orders +5% vs budget — a relative bright spot within retail.

qa.question: How did American Golf do versus budget last week?
qa.answer: Total AGT despatched sales were £2,844k in Week 19, 16.9% behind budget and -17.5% LFL. Month-to-date is £6,112k, -12.5% vs budget.

qa.question: Is this a margin problem or a volume problem?
qa.answer: Volume. Retail transactions fell sharply year-on-year while ATV and ASP rose, and the total margin rate actually held at 42.2% (+0.4pts vs budget). It's a footfall/demand problem, not product or pricing.

qa.question: What's the biggest risk to the forecast?
qa.answer: The N6W run-rate flags a sales risk of about £1.7m, with Clubs the largest single risk at c.£1.06m (Custom, Irons and Woods). Cash-margin run-rate risk is c.£0.59m.

qa.question: How is ecommerce performing?
qa.answer: Web was -9% vs budget on sales in Week 19 but its margin rate is +2.8pts YoY and L4W category sales are well up year-on-year across Clubs, Equipment and Clothing. It's the channel to accelerate.

qa.question: How are the new store openings doing?
qa.answer: Blanchardstown is -64.9% versus target YTD and Go Northampton -54.2%; both are well behind. Chichester is the exception, running +15% ahead of target.

qa.question: What are customers complaining about?
qa.answer: Delivery delays (especially via EVRI), stock availability and incorrect pricing. The TrustScore is steady at 4.7 across 175k reviews. Customer service handled 2,325 contacts, led by dispatch, delivery and refund tickets.

qa.question: Is there anything urgent or operational I should know?
qa.answer: Yes — a vishing (fraudulent IT-support call) campaign targeted stores; 7 were contacted and around 30 remain to verify. Separately, Belfast civil unrest forced a temporary store closure.

qa.question: How is custom fit doing?
qa.answer: Holding up well — custom-fit appointments were +14% LFL in Week 19 (in-store +19%) and custom orders +5% vs budget.

narrative.text: Sales, Margin & KPI Tracker - Dispatched. FY 2026 / 2027 - June. AGT (Retail & Ecomm), Weeks 18-21, Total to Date and Full Month BUD.
Week 19 is the latest actual week; Weeks 20 and 21 show Budget/Forecast and Last Year only.

summary: Headline: In Week 19 American Golf (Retail & Ecomm) delivered £2,813k net sales, -17.0% vs budget and -17.0% on 1-year LFL, with margin held at 42.0% (+0.2pts vs budget, +0.7pts YoY). The story is volume, not value: retail transactions were -27.5% LFL while ATV (+9.5% LFL) and ASP (+11.6% YoY) both rose. Ecommerce is more resilient (-4.3% LFL but strongly up YoY, mix 24.7%), and own-brand mix at 20.9% (+2.4pts YoY) is supporting margin. Watch: custom-order redemptions -21.4% and gift-card sales -43% in the week.

insights.headline: Sales -17% vs budget, but margin protected
insights.detail: Week 19 AGT net sales £2,813k, -17.0% vs budget and -17.0% 1-yr LFL, yet margin rate held at 42.0% — +0.2pts vs budget and +0.7pts YoY. The shortfall is demand, not profitability.

insights.headline: Footfall down, basket up
insights.detail: Retail transactions were -27.5% on LFL in Week 19, but ATV rose +9.5% LFL and ASP +11.6% YoY. Fewer customers, each spending more — a footfall problem.

insights.headline: Ecommerce more resilient than retail
insights.detail: Ecomm net sales were -4.3% LFL in Week 19 but well up year-on-year, with ecomm mix at 24.7% and margin 39.5% (+1.5pts YoY). Web is holding the line better than stores.

insights.headline: Own-brand mix lifting margin
insights.detail: Total own-brand mix reached 20.9% in Week 19, +2.4pts YoY, with clothing own-brand particularly strong — a key support to the steady margin rate.

qa.question: What were last week's sales versus budget?
qa.answer: Week 19 AGT net sales were £2,813k, -17.0% versus budget and -17.0% on 1-year LFL.

qa.question: Did margin hold up?
qa.answer: Yes — the Week 19 margin rate was 42.0%, +0.2pts versus budget and +0.7pts year-on-year.

qa.question: Footfall or spend — which is the problem?
qa.answer: Footfall. Retail transactions were -27.5% on LFL, while ATV (+9.5% LFL) and ASP (+11.6% YoY) both rose.

qa.question: How is ecommerce doing?
qa.answer: More resilient than retail: -4.3% LFL in Week 19 but strongly up year-on-year, with ecomm mix at 24.7% and a 39.5% margin rate.
